stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The pipeline indicates its primary use case is text generation.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core area of text generation within the AI/ML community.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: School shooting survivor sues AI gun detection firm after system failed to spot weapon
stories.author: Cyrus Farivar
bullet_points: A survivor of a school shooting is suing an AI gun detection firm.
The lawsuit centers on the AI system's failure to detect a weapon in a critical situation.
The story raises significant ethical and practical questions regarding the required accuracy of life-saving AI systems.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes key ML technologies such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like transformers and pytorch.
The pipeline indicates its primary use case is text generation.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, aiming for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is connecting messaging workflows directly to local, private AI capabilities.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run coding assistants (like Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud models.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: S&P 500 is reportedly blocking SpaceX from gaining easy access to passive investor capital.
The exclusion also extends to major AI players, specifically OpenAI and Anthropic.
This indicates a potential shift in how large, high-growth, or non-traditional tech companies are integrated into mainstream investment indices.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Code2LoRA introduces a hypernetwork framework to generate repository-specific LoRA adapters for code language models.
This method addresses the challenge of injecting large-scale repository context (imports, APIs) without incurring high costs or inference-time overhead.
It offers a more scalable and robust alternative to traditional RAG or per-repository fine-tuning when dealing with evolving codebases.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a novel LLM-based, self-evolving multi-agent framework for Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) algorithm discovery.
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, specifically inter-branch information isolation, memoryless search, and poor hierarchical control.
Positions self-evolution as a key capability for LLM agents tackling complex, long-horizon tasks like scientific discovery and MLE engineering.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Introduces Code2LoRA, a hypernetwork framework designed to inject repository-level context into Code Language Models.
Addresses the limitations of current methods (long inputs/per-repository fine-tuning) which are costly and brittle to code evolution.
Achieves repository knowledge injection with zero inference-time token overhead by generating specific LoRA adapters.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a self-evolving, multi-agent framework designed for automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery (MLE).
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, specifically inter-branch information isolation, memoryless search, and poor hierarchical control.
Positions the work at the intersection of LLM agents and long-horizon tasks like scientific discovery and advanced ML engineering.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across diverse hardware setups.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving functionality on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run on user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, integrating with local LLM ecosystems.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM tools and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A USB-connected speaker has been shown to potentially infect a PC without physical user interaction.
The specific device mentioned is the Sound Blaster Katana V2X.
The seller of the device does not classify the observed behavior as a security vulnerability.

stories.title: Small modular nuclear reactor reaches criticality in first test
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: A startup named Antares has achieved criticality with a small modular nuclear reactor (SMR).
The reactor is currently in an early testing phase and is not yet capable of generating power.
This represents a significant step forward in the development of advanced nuclear energy technology.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Introduces HANDOFF, a new framework for humanoid robot control.
Addresses the critical issue of bridging high-level task planning with low-level whole-body control.
Proposes a compact, explicit interface to overcome the limitations of dense kinematic references in existing controllers.

stories.title: TempoVLA: Learning Speed-Controllable Vision-Language-Action Policies
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: TempoVLA addresses a key limitation in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models: the inability to control execution speed dynamically.
The research tackles the need for robots to switch between fast transit phases and slow, precise contact stages during manipulation.
Unlike prior methods that only change between fixed speeds, TempoVLA specifically explores and enables controlled deceleration.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks, which fail to account for progressive human-AI co-editing.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during revision.
Highlights the growing real-world complexity of documents resulting from integrated human-AI drafting workflows.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), specifically issues with sequential computation and gradient instability.
It introduces Supervised Memory Training (SMT), a novel method designed to train nonlinear RNNs.
SMT circumvents the need for traditional recurrent credit propagation, potentially improving parallelism and long-range dependency learning.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning for reasoning language models.
It introduces RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve RL steering for Chain-of-Thought (CoT) traces.
The work targets limitations of existing methods like GRPO, which rely on Monte Carlo approaches where rewards are only available after the full trace is complete.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a novel method for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) by utilizing discarded tokens from Discrete Diffusion Language Models.
Discrete diffusion models generate text by iteratively denoising the entire response in parallel, predicting tentative tokens at each step.
The discarded, low-confidence tokens serve as a valuable 'lookahead signal' to guide and strengthen the retrieval process.

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method allows preconditioned weights to be merged back post-training with zero inference overhead.

stories.title: TailLoR: Protecting Principal Components in Parameter-Efficient Continual Learning
stories.author: Florin Brad
bullet_points: Introduces TailLoR, a new parameter-efficient finetuning method for Continual Learning.
It uses the singular bases (U and V) of pre-trained weights as a fixed reference frame for low-rank updates.
A soft spectral penalty is employed to prevent updates from interfering with dominant singular directions, focusing adaptation on 'long-tail' components.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Introduces HANDOFF, a new framework for whole-body control in humanoid robots.
Addresses the critical challenge of bridging high-level task planning with low-level robot control.
Proposes a compact, explicit, and intuitive interface to replace dense kinematic references.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks, which fail to account for progressive human-AI co-editing workflows.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during text revision.
Relevant to the increasing integration of AI writing assistants into real-world drafting and revision processes.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: Addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs).
Proposes Supervised Memory Training (SMT) to bypass the need for recurrent credit propagation.
Aims to improve long-range association learning by eliminating sequential time dependencies, potentially boosting parallelism.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a novel method, Self-Augmenting Retrieval, for Diffusion Language Models.
It leverages 'discarded' low-confidence tokens from the denoising process as a valuable lookahead signal.
This signal enhances Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) by enabling the retrieval of stronger evidence.

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method allows for merging preconditioned weights back into the original architecture with no inference overhead.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks, which fail to account for progressive human-AI co-editing.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during revision.
Highlights the growing real-world challenge of documents being a hybrid result of human and AI input.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a method to leverage 'discarded' low-confidence tokens from discrete diffusion language models.
These discarded tokens are shown to act as a useful 'lookahead signal' for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
The technique aims to improve the retrieval process by surfacing salient entities early in the denoising trajectory of the model.

stories.title: You Only Index Once: Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing
stories.author: Furu Wei
bullet_points: The research addresses the efficiency bottleneck of long-context inference in Large Language Models (LLMs).
It proposes 'Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing' to mitigate the quality-efficiency trade-off in sparse attention methods.
The work targets improving decoding efficiency, particularly for reasoning-heavy tasks requiring long chains of thought.

stories.title: Scaffold, Not Vocabulary? A Controlled, Two-Tier, Pre-Registered Study of a Popperian Code-Generation Skill
stories.author: Mehmet Iscan
bullet_points: The study investigates the effectiveness of 'Popperian' prompting techniques to improve code generation by Large Language Models (LLMs).
It critically examines the reliance on LLM-as-a-judge systems, which are known to possess inherent biases (positional, self-preference, stylistic).
The research questions whether observed improvements in code quality are genuinely due to the prompting skill or artifacts of the evaluation method.

stories.title: Latent Reasoning with Normalizing Flows
stories.author: Jiatao Gu
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitations of Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting in LLMs, noting that verbalizing reasoning steps is restrictive.
It proposes 'Latent Reasoning' as a higher-bandwidth alternative to textual CoT.
Latent reasoning allows for intermediate computation to occur in a continuous, semantic space rather than a discrete token stream.

stories.title: USAD 2.0: Scaling Representation Distillation for Universal Audio Understanding
stories.author: James Glass
bullet_points: Introduces USAD 2.0, a new universal audio encoder designed to handle diverse audio inputs.
Addresses the limitation of current domain-specific audio encoders by integrating knowledge from both Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) and supervised methods.
Relevant to the trend of using unified encoders within large language models (LLMs) for multimodal understanding.

stories.title: Revising Context, Shifting Simulated Stance: Auditing LLM-Based Stance Simulation in Online Discussions
stories.author: Jiebo Luo
bullet_points: The research addresses the reliability of LLMs used to simulate online user stances in discussions.
It introduces 'counterfactual context revision' as a framework to audit these LLM simulations.
The study investigates whether LLM simulations accurately reflect user beliefs or are overly sensitive to minor context changes.

stories.title: Show HN: Lathe – Use LLMs to learn a new domain, not skip past it
stories.author: devenjarvis
bullet_points: Lathe is an experimental tool designed to use LLMs for active learning rather than automated task completion.
It generates hands-on, source-backed tutorials for technical topics, requiring the user to code manually in a local UI.
The tool leverages LLM agent skills (like Claude Code/Cursor) and is accessible via a Go CLI.

stories.title: Run Coding Agents on Local AI — Zero Cloud, Full Control
stories.author: dalenguyen
bullet_points: The article discusses the growing utility of coding agents (e.g., Codex CLI, Cursor) as productivity tools.
The core topic is running these AI coding agents locally, emphasizing a 'Zero Cloud, Full Control' approach.
This shift suggests a trend towards on-premise or local execution of powerful AI tools for enhanced data privacy and control.

stories.title: I Built a 5-Agent AI System That Fixes Kubernetes Clusters Before Your Pager Goes Off
stories.author: sodiqjimoh
bullet_points: The article details the creation of a 5-agent AI system for autonomous Kubernetes cluster self-healing.
It leverages the Qwen family of models (Max, Embedding, Turbo) for its functionality.
The system claims to resolve 80% of operational incidents without requiring human intervention.
